entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop studied for its susceptibility to soil water deficits at different growth stages.

entities.name: WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: The stress condition of soil water shortage imposed on the crop.

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The research method used to determine crop susceptibility over a 2-year period.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The measure of productivity used to determine the extent of loss due to water stress.

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: A critical growth stage used to determine the timing of stress termination and its effect on yield.

entities.name: GRAIN FILLING
entities.description: A growth stage where the relief of water stress has a minimal adverse effect on grain yield.

entities.name: TILLERS
entities.description: Plant structures that can produce panicles to compensate for yield loss when stress is relieved early.

entities.name: PANICLES
entities.description: The reproductive structures produced by tillers.

entities.name: MID-SEASON DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Water deficit occurring during the middle of the crop growth cycle.

entities.name: LATE-SEASON DROUGHT
entities.description: Water deficit occurring during the late stages of crop growth.

relationships.description: Susceptibility of the crop to soil water deficits was determined.
Field experiments were conducted to study the crop.
Water deficit leads to loss in grain yield.
The time of stress termination from flowering determines the extent of grain yield loss.
Water stress relieved at early grain filling has little adverse effect on grain yield.
Additional tillers produce panicles to mitigate yield loss.
The response of tillers affects the overall grain yield.
Mid-season drought is a specific timing of water deficit.
Late-season drought is a specific timing of water deficit.
Susceptibility to mid-season drought is related to the time stress was terminated.
Susceptibility to late-season drought is related to the time stress was initiated.
